blockingIssues.description: `session_input_callback` provenance still falls back to history-first frequency matching for duplicate-valued JSON inputs whose identity is not tracked (objects, numbers, booleans, and null), so callback-shaped persistence can still misattribute new-turn items to history.

`MCPServerManager::connect_all()` restores only the previously connected subset on strict failure, so previously active failed servers are still dropped when `drop_failed_servers = false` instead of preserving the full prior active set.

`contains_machine_local_absolute_path` still misses forward-slash Windows drive absolute paths such as `D:/a/...`, so the docs contract remains narrower than the generic machine-local absolute-path rejection expected by the audit blocker.

rejectedObservations.observation: The shared `docs_audit` command omits `docs_contract` even though the docs portability and README harness are part of the audit surface.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected as an ambiguous services.yaml change for the validator to apply directly: the manifest already exposes `docs_contract` separately, and changing the semantics of the existing `docs_audit` entry would be a non-additive naming/ownership decision rather than a low-risk factual update.
